Isaiah was a sign to the people by his unusual dress when he walked abroad. He commonly wore sackcloth as a prophet to show himself humbled before the world. He was to remove this from his loins, wear no upper garments, and go barefooted. This sign was to signify that the Egyptians and Ethiopians would be led away captive by the king of Assyria, thus stripped. The world will often deem believers foolish when they are singular in obedience to God. But the Lord will support his servants under the most trying effects of their obedience; and what they are called upon to suffer for his sake is commonly light compared with what numbers groan under from year to year from sin. Those who make any creature their expectation and glory, and so put it in the place of God, will sooner or later be ashamed of it. But disappointment in creature-confidences should not drive us to despair; instead, it should drive us to God, and our expectation shall not be in vain. The same lesson is in force now: where shall we look for aid in the hour of necessity but to the Lord our Righteousness?
